reasoning: The 4-week moving average of seasonally adjusted insured unemployment claims has shown strong upward momentum recently, increasing from 1.922 million to 1.947 million over the past month. For the shortest horizon (Nov 2, roughly 1 week out), the moving average is extremely likely to continue increasing because it will drop a lower four-week-old value, and recent high weekly numbers will dominate the average. The linear trend projection strongly supports an increase over the next week. For the middle horizon (Nov 25, roughly 4 weeks out), uncertainty increases, but the upward trend gives it a positive bias. For the longest horizon (Jan 2026, 3 months out), uncertainty dominates and we regress heavily toward the base rate of ~0.5.
